Program:
```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size and basic helpers
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.
dummy(R,C) :- R = R.
no_input(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,_).
has_input(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Find the maximum (outer) colour that appears in the input
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_clue(C) :- input(R,C,C), C = C.   % make R and C safe
max_clue(ClueMax) :- ClueMax = #max { C : has_clue(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Columns that contain a cell of maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_has_max(C) :- input(R,C,ClueMax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Define the outer “plus‑sign” shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C) :- R = 5.
shape(R,C) :- col_has_max(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Compute the extreme columns of the maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_range_min(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min { C : col_has_max(C) }.
col_range_max(Ccolmax) :- Ccolmax = #max { C : col_has_max(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior region (strictly between the two extreme columns,
%    and not the middle row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- R != 5,
                C > Cmin,
                C < Ccolmax,
                col_range_min(Cmin),
                col_range_max(Ccolmax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Which colour is allowed for each cell?
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) a clue on the outer shape – keep that clue
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, shape(R,C), dummy(R,C).

% b) a clue inside the interior region – it becomes the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), interior(R,C), V = ClueMax, dummy(R,C).

% c) a clue outside the interior region – keep its own colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = V, dummy(R,C).

% d) a cell without a clue that lies on the outer shape – become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), shape(R,C), V = ClueMax, dummy(R,C).

% e) a cell that is neither on the shape nor interior – stay empty
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), not shape(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = 0, dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell, respecting the above allowed set
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Uniqueness via minimising the total colour sum
% ------------------------------------------------------------
sum_output(S) :- S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size and basic helpers
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.

% safe dummy predicate
dummy(R,C) :- R = R, C = C.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Maximal colour with safe binding
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% max_clue(ClueMax,R) holds when ClueMax is the maximum column index C
% that has a clue in column C for the given row R.
max_clue(ClueMax,R) :- R = 0..15, ClueMax = #max { C : input(R,C,C), C = C }.

% a column C contains the maximal colour if there is a row R with
% a clue of that colour.
col_has_max(C,R) :- max_clue(ClueMax,R), input(R,C,ClueMax), dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Define the outer “plus‑sign” shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C,Rmax) :- R = 5, C = C, dummy(R,C), max_clue(ClueMax,R), input(R,C,ClueMax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Extreme columns of maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_range_min(Cmin,R) :- Cmin = #min { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.
col_range_max(Ccolmax,R) :- Ccolmax = #max { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Interior region (strictly between the two extreme columns,
%    and not the middle row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    R != 5,
    C > Cmin,
    C < Cmax,
    col_range_min(Cmin,R),
    col_range_max(Cmax,R),
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Allowed colours per cell (respecting negation safety)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) clue on the outer shape – keep that clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    dummy(R,C).

% b) clue inside interior – it becomes maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) }, % bind maximal colour locally
    dummy(R,C).

% c) clue outside interior – keep its own colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = V,
    dummy(R,C).

% d) cell without a clue on the outer shape – become maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) },
    dummy(R,C).

% e) cell empty – stay empty
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = 0,
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Sum for optional optimisation (unused here)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% sum_output(S) :- S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size and basic helpers
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% safe dummy predicate (both variables appear positively)
dummy(R,C)  :- R = R, C = C.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Basic auxiliary predicates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a cell that contains a clue whose colour equals the column index
has_input(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Maximal colour (with a safe binding for the row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% max_clue(ClueMax,R) holds when ClueMax is the maximum column index
% that has a clue in column ClueMax for the given row R.
max_clue(ClueMax,R) :- R = 0..15, ClueMax = #max { C : input(R,C,C), C = C }.

% a column C contains the maximal colour for the same row R
col_has_max(C,R) :- max_clue(ClueMax,R), input(R,C,ClueMax), dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. The outer “plus‑sign” shape (both R and C are positively bound)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C,Rmax) :-
    R = 5,
    C = C,
    dummy(R,C),
    max_clue(ClueMax,Rmax),
    input(R,C,ClueMax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Extreme columns of the maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_range_min(Cmin,R) :- Cmin = #min { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.
col_range_max(Cmax,R) :- Cmax = #max { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior region (strictly between the extremes, not the middle row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    R != 5,
    C > Cmin,
    C < Cmax,
    col_range_min(Cmin,R),
    col_range_max(Cmax,R),
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Allowed colours for each cell (negations are safe)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) clue on the outer shape – keep that clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    dummy(R,C).

% b) clue inside the interior – it becomes the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) },
    dummy(R,C).

% c) clue outside the interior – keep its own colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = V,
    dummy(R,C).

% d) cell without a clue on the outer shape – become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) },
    dummy(R,C).

% e) a cell that is neither on the shape nor interior – stay empty
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = 0,
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell (respecting the allowed set)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Optional sum for optimisation (safe version)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
sum_output(S,R,C) :-
    R = 0..15,
    C = 0..15,
    dummy(R,C),
    S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.

rewrite_details.program_before:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size and basic helpers
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.
dummy(R,C) :- R = R.
no_input(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,_).
has_input(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Find the maximum (outer) colour that appears in the input
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_clue(C) :- input(R,C,C), C = C.   % make R and C safe
max_clue(ClueMax) :- ClueMax = #max { C : has_clue(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Columns that contain a cell of maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_has_max(C) :- input(R,C,ClueMax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Define the outer “plus‑sign” shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C) :- R = 5.
shape(R,C) :- col_has_max(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Compute the extreme columns of the maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_range_min(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min { C : col_has_max(C) }.
col_range_max(Ccolmax) :- Ccolmax = #max { C : col_has_max(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior region (strictly between the two extreme columns,
%    and not the middle row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- R != 5,
                C > Cmin,
                C < Ccolmax,
                col_range_min(Cmin),
                col_range_max(Ccolmax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Which colour is allowed for each cell?
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) a clue on the outer shape – keep that clue
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, shape(R,C), dummy(R,C).

% b) a clue inside the interior region – it becomes the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), interior(R,C), V = ClueMax, dummy(R,C).

% c) a clue outside the interior region – keep its own colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = V, dummy(R,C).

% d) a cell without a clue that lies on the outer shape – become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), shape(R,C), V = ClueMax, dummy(R,C).

% e) a cell that is neither on the shape nor interior – stay empty
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), not shape(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = 0, dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell, respecting the above allowed set
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Uniqueness via minimising the total colour sum
% ------------------------------------------------------------
sum_output(S) :- S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Helper predicates for rows and columns
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.
no_input(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,_).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Determine the maximal clue color
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_clue(C) :- input(R,C,C).
max_clue = #max { C : has_clue(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Columns that contain the maximal clue
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_has_max(C) :- input(R,C,max_clue).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Shape of the outer border: middle row (5) and all columns
%    that have a maximal clue.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C) :- R = 5.
shape(R,C) :- col_has_max(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Allowed output colors for each cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) If the cell contains a clue, its output must be that clue.
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% b) Cells without a clue that lie on the outer shape
%    become the maximal clue color.
allowed(R,C,V) :- no_input(R,C), shape(R,C), V = max_clue.

% c) Remaining cells (no clue, not on shape) become 0.
allowed(R,C,V) :- no_input(R,C), not shape(R,C), V = 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Exactly one output per cell, respecting the allowed set
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- R = 0..15, C = 0..15.
```

Thus final program:

- define row, col, no_input, has_input.

- define has_clue(C) :- input(R,C,C).

- max_clue = #max { C : has_clue(C) }.

- col_has_max(C) :- input(R,C,max_clue).

- shape(R,C) :- R = 5; shape(R,C) :- col_has_max(C).

- has_input(R,C) :- not no_input(R,C).

- allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, shape(R,C). % clue on shape

- allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), V = max_clue. % clue off shape

- allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), shape(R,C), V = max_clue. % shape no clue

- allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), not shape(R,C), V = 0. % non-shape non-clue

- sum constraint: min_sum = #min { S : S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) } }.

- sum_output = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.

- sum_output = min_sum.

- cell(R,C) :- R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% Row and column ranges
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.

% No input at a cell
no_input(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,_).

% Has input (i.e., a clue) at the cell
has_input(R,C) :- not no_input(R,C).

% Find the maximal clue colour present in the input
has_clue(C) :- input(R,C,C).
max_clue = #max { C : has_clue(C) }.

% Columns that contain a maximal clue cell
col_has_max(C) :- input(R,C,max_clue).

% Define the outer shape: the middle row (row 5) and all columns that contain the maximal clue
shape(R,C) :- R = 5.
shape(R,C) :- col_has_max(C).

% Compute the extreme columns where the maximal clue occurs
col_range_min = #min { C : col_has_max(C) }.
col_range_max = #max { C : col_has_max(C) }.

% Interior region: rows different from the middle row and columns strictly between the two extreme columns
interior(R,C) :- R != 5, C > col_range_min, C < col_range_max.

% Allowed output colours per cell
% a) clue cells that lie on the outer shape keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, shape(R,C).

% b) clue cells off the outer shape but inside the interior region become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), interior(R,C), V = max_clue.

% c) clue cells off the outer shape and outside the interior region keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = V.

% d) shape cells that have no clue become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), shape(R,C), V = max_clue.

% e) cells that are neither on the outer shape nor in the interior region become 0
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), not shape(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = 0.

% Exactly one colour per cell, respecting the allowed set
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% Enforce that the solution uses the minimal possible total colour sum
sum_output = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.
min_sum = #min { S : S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) } }.
sum_output = min_sum.

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size and basic helpers
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)    :- R = 0..15.
col(C)    :- C = 0..15.
no_input(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,_).
has_input(R,C) :- not no_input(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Find the maximum (outer) colour that appears in the input
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_clue(C)      :- input(R,C,C).
max_clue = #max { C : has_clue(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Columns that contain a cell of maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_has_max(C) :- input(R,C,max_clue).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Define the outer “plus‑sign” shape
%    – the central row (row 5)  OR  a column that contains a maximal clue
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C) :- R = 5.
shape(R,C) :- col_has_max(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Compute the extreme columns of the maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_range_min = #min { C : col_has_max(C) }.
col_range_max = #max { C : col_has_max(C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior region (strictly between the two extreme columns,
%    and not the middle row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- R != 5, C > col_range_min, C < col_range_max.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Which colour is allowed for each cell?
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) a clue on the outer shape – keep that clue
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, shape(R,C).

% b) a clue inside the interior region – it becomes the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), interior(R,C), V = max_clue.

% c) a clue outside the interior region – keep its own colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not shape(R,C), has_input(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = V.

% d) a cell without a clue that lies on the outer shape – become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), shape(R,C), V = max_clue.

% e) a cell that is neither on the shape nor interior – stay empty
allowed(R,C,V) :- not input(R,C,_), not shape(R,C), not interior(R,C), V = 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell, respecting the above allowed set
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Uniqueness via minimising the total colour sum
% ------------------------------------------------------------
sum_output = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.
min_sum    = #min { S : S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) } }.
sum_output = min_sum.

refinements.program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size and basic helpers
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% safe dummy predicate (both variables appear positively)
dummy(R,C)  :- R = R, C = C.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Basic auxiliary predicates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a cell that contains a clue whose colour equals the column index
has_input(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Maximal colour (with a safe binding for the row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% max_clue(ClueMax,R) holds when ClueMax is the maximum column index
% that has a clue in column ClueMax for the given row R.
max_clue(ClueMax,R) :- R = 0..15, ClueMax = #max { C : input(R,C,C), C = C }.

% a column C contains the maximal colour for the same row R
col_has_max(C,R) :- max_clue(ClueMax,R), input(R,C,ClueMax), dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. The outer “plus‑sign” shape (both R and C are positively bound)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C,Rmax) :-
    R = 5,
    C = C,
    dummy(R,C),
    max_clue(ClueMax,Rmax),
    input(R,C,ClueMax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Extreme columns of the maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_range_min(Cmin,R) :- Cmin = #min { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.
col_range_max(Cmax,R) :- Cmax = #max { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior region (strictly between the extremes, not the middle row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    R != 5,
    C > Cmin,
    C < Cmax,
    col_range_min(Cmin,R),
    col_range_max(Cmax,R),
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Allowed colours for each cell (negations are safe)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) clue on the outer shape – keep that clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    dummy(R,C).

% b) clue inside the interior – it becomes the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) },
    dummy(R,C).

% c) clue outside the interior – keep its own colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = V,
    dummy(R,C).

% d) cell without a clue on the outer shape – become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) },
    dummy(R,C).

% e) a cell that is neither on the shape nor interior – stay empty
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = 0,
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell (respecting the allowed set)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Optional sum for optimisation (safe version)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
sum_output(S,R,C) :-
    R = 0..15,
    C = 0..15,
    dummy(R,C),
    S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates (note the extra
%    column predicate in the head!)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin),
                    Len = #int { Cmax - Cmin }, col(Cmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin,C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax,C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax,C), col_min(Rmin,C),
                       Len = #int { Rmax - Rmin }, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(MaxLen,Rmax) :- row(Rmax), row_len(Rmax,Len), Len = #max { Len : row_len(R,Len) }.
central_row(R)      :- row(R), row_len(R,Len), Len = #max { Len : row_len(R,Len) }.

max_col_len(MaxLen,Cmax) :- col(Cmax), col_len(Rmin,Rmax,Cmax), Len = #max { Len : col_len(Rmin,Rmax,Cmax) }.
central_col(C)      :- col(C), col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C), Len = #max { Len : col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the
%    central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R,Cmin), row_max(R,Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Colour of a column – the maximal colour that occurs
%    in that column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_colour(C,V) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), V = #max { V2 : clue(R2,C,V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell – the allowed set decides
%    which colour is allowed.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) cells on the central row or column keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    (central_row(R) ; central_col(C)),
    row(R), col(C).

% b) interior cells take the maximal colour of their column
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_colour(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

% c) all other cells stay empty
allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements – the program produces exactly one
%     answer set which matches the specification.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin), Len = Cmax - Cmin, col(Cmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, Len) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax, C), col_min(Rmin, C), Len = Rmax - Rmin, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(Rmax, MaxLen) :- row(Rmax), MaxLen = #max { Len : row_len(Rmax, Len) }.
central_row(R) :- row(R), Len = #max { Len : row_len(R, Len) }.

max_col_len(Cmax, MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { Len : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, Cmax, Len) }.
central_col(C) :- col(C), Len = #max { Len : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, Len) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the
%    central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R, Cmin), row_max(R, Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Colour of a column – the maximal colour that occurs
%    in that column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C, V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R, C, V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell – the allowed set decides
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) cells on the central row or column keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    (central_row(R) ; central_col(C)),
    row(R), col(C).

% b) interior cells take the maximal colour of their column
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

% c) all other cells stay empty
allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements – the program produces exactly one
%     answer set which matches the specification.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin), Len = Cmax - Cmin, col(Cmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, Len) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax, C), col_min(Rmin, C), Len = Rmax - Rmin, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(Rmax, MaxLen) :- row(Rmax), MaxLen = #max { L : row_len(Rmax, L) }.
central_row(R) :- row(R), Len = L, L = #max { L : row_len(R, L) }.

max_col_len(Cmax, MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, Cmax, L) }.
central_col(C) :- col(C), Len = L, L = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, L) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the
%    central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R, Cmin), row_max(R, Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Colour of a column – the maximal colour that occurs
%    in that column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C, V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R, C, V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell – the allowed set decides
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) cells on the central row or column keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    (central_row(R) ; central_col(C)),
    row(R), col(C).

% b) interior cells take the maximal colour of their column
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

% c) all other cells stay empty
allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements – the program produces exactly one
%     answer set which matches the specification.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin), Len = Cmax - Cmin, col(Cmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, Len) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax, C), col_min(Rmin, C), Len = Rmax - Rmin, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(Rmax, MaxLen) :- row(Rmax), MaxLen = #max { L : row_len(Rmax, L) }.
central_row(R) :- row(R), Len = L, L = #max { L : row_len(R, L) }.

max_col_len(Cmax, MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, Cmax, L) }.
central_col(C) :- col(C), Len = L, L = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, L) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the
%    central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R, Cmin), row_max(R, Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Colour of a column – the maximal colour that occurs
%    in that column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C, V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R, C, V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell – the allowed set decides
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) cells on the central row or column keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    (central_row(R) ; central_col(C)),
    row(R), col(C).

% b) interior cells take the maximal colour of their column
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

% c) all other cells stay empty
allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements – the program produces exactly one
%     answer set which matches the specification.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,_) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,_) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin), Len = Cmax - Cmin.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin, C) :- col(C), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,_) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax, C) :- col(C), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,_) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, Len) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax, C), col_min(Rmin, C), Len = Rmax - Rmin, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(Rmax, MaxLen) :- row(Rmax), MaxLen = #max { L : row_len(Rmax, L) }.
central_row(R)   :- row(R), Len = #max { L : row_len(R, L) }.

max_col_len(Cmax, MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, Cmax, L) }.
central_col(C)   :- col(C), Len = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, L) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R, Cmin), row_max(R, Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Max colour in a column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C, V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R, C, _) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Allowed colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
allowed_center(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    row(R), col(C),
    central_row(R) ; central_col(C).   % clue kept on central row or column

allowed_interior(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

allowed_empty(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed_center(R,C,V) ; allowed_interior(R,C,V) ; allowed_empty(R,C,V) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin), Len = Cmax - Cmin.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, Len) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax, C), col_min(Rmin, C), Len = Rmax - Rmin, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(Rmax, MaxLen) :- row(Rmax), MaxLen = #max { L : row_len(Rmax, L) }.
central_row(R)   :- row(R), Len = #max { L : row_len(R, L) }.

max_col_len(Cmax, MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, Cmax, L) }.
central_col(C)   :- col(C), Len = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, L) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R, Cmin), row_max(R, Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Max colour in a column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C, V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R, C, V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Allowed colour per cell – three mutually exclusive cases
% ------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    row(R), col(C),
    central_row(R) ; central_col(C).

allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements
% ------------------------------------------------------------

rewrite_details.program_before:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin), Len = Cmax - Cmin, col(Cmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax, C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, Len) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax, C), col_min(Rmin, C), Len = Rmax - Rmin, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(Rmax, MaxLen) :- row(Rmax), MaxLen = #max { L : row_len(Rmax, L) }.
central_row(R) :- row(R), Len = L, L = #max { L : row_len(R, L) }.

max_col_len(Cmax, MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, Cmax, L) }.
central_col(C) :- col(C), Len = L, L = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, L) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the
%    central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R, Cmin), row_max(R, Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Colour of a column – the maximal colour that occurs
%    in that column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C, V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R, C, V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell – the allowed set decides
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) cells on the central row or column keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    (central_row(R) ; central_col(C)),
    row(R), col(C).

% b) interior cells take the maximal colour of their column
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

% c) all other cells stay empty
allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements – the program produces exactly one
%     answer set which matches the specification.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

Common Clingo syntax fixes (apply immediately):
  aggregate(N=#count,cond,N)    →  N = #count { _ : cond }
  aggregate_all(#count,cond,N)  →  N = #count { _ : cond }
  C #mod 2                      →  C \ 2
  #min{...} = Var               →  Var = #min{...}
  { } exactly N :-              →  N { } N :-
  #count(...)                   →  #count{...}

  #const UPPER = N.             →  #const upper = N.
  X = #max{...}.  (standalone)  →  max_val(X) :- X = #max{...}.
  #count{A} = #count{B} (body)  →  N = #count{A}, N = #count{B}  (bind one side first)

central_row(R)   :- row(R), Len = #max { L : row_len(R, L) }.

max_col_len(Cmax, MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, Cmax, L) }.
central_col(C)   :- col(C), Len = #max { L : col_len(Rmin, Rmax, C, L) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R, Cmin), row_max(R, Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Max colour in a column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C, V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R, C, V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Allowed colour per cell – three mutually exclusive cases
% ------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    row(R), col(C),
    (central_row(R) ; central_col(C)).   % clue kept on central row or column

allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements
% ------------------------------------------------------------

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R,Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R,Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R,Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R,Cmax), row_min(R,Cmin), Len = Cmax - Cmin.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin,C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax,C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C,Len) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax,C), col_min(Rmin,C), Len = Rmax - Rmin, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(Rmax,MaxLen) :- row(Rmax), MaxLen = #max { L : row_len(Rmax,L) }.
central_row(R) :- row(R), Len = #max { L : row_len(R,L) }.

max_col_len(Cmax,MaxLen) :- col(Cmax), MaxLen = #max { L : col_len(Rmin,Rmax,Cmax,L) }.
central_col(C) :- col(C), Len = #max { L : col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C,L) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R,Cmin),
    row_max(R,Cmax),
    C >= Cmin,
    C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Max colour in a column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_color(C,V) :- col(C), V = #max { V2 : clue(R,C,V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Allowed colour per cell – three mutually exclusive cases
% ------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    row(R), col(C),
    (central_row(R); central_col(C)).

allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_color(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

output_cell(R,C) :- max_row(Rmax), max_col(Cmax),

% ------------------------------------------------------------

output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not has_color(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. No extra aggregates – all uses of counting are of the form

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size and basic helpers
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% safe dummy predicate (both variables appear positively)
dummy(R,C)  :- R = R, C = C.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Basic auxiliary predicates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a cell that contains a clue whose colour equals the column index
has_input(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Maximal colour (with a safe binding for the row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% max_clue(ClueMax,R) holds when ClueMax is the maximum column index
% that has a clue in column ClueMax for the given row R.
max_clue(ClueMax,R) :- R = 0..15, ClueMax = #max { C : input(R,C,C), C = C }.

% a column C contains the maximal colour for the same row R
col_has_max(C,R) :- max_clue(ClueMax,R), input(R,C,ClueMax), dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. The outer “plus‑sign” shape (both R and C are positively bound)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shape(R,C,Rmax) :-
    R = 5,
    C = C,
    dummy(R,C),
    max_clue(ClueMax,Rmax),
    input(R,C,ClueMax).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Extreme columns of the maximal colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_range_min(Cmin,R) :- Cmin = #min { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.
col_range_max(Cmax,R) :- Cmax = #max { C : col_has_max(C,R), dummy(R,C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior region (strictly between the extremes, not the middle row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    R != 5,
    C > Cmin,
    C < Cmax,
    col_range_min(Cmin,R),
    col_range_max(Cmax,R),
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Allowed colours for each cell (negations are safe)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) clue on the outer shape – keep that clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    dummy(R,C).

% b) clue inside the interior – it becomes the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) },
    dummy(R,C).

% c) clue outside the interior – keep its own colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    has_input(R,C),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = V,
    dummy(R,C).

% d) cell without a clue on the outer shape – become the maximal colour
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    shape(R,C,Rmax),
    V = #max { Col : max_clue(Col,R) },
    dummy(R,C).

% e) a cell that is neither on the shape nor interior – stay empty
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    not input(R,C,_),
    not shape(R,C,Rmax),
    not interior(R,C,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    V = 0,
    dummy(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell (respecting the allowed set)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    R = 0..15, C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Optional sum for optimisation (safe version)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
sum_output(S,R,C) :-
    R = 0..15,
    C = 0..15,
    dummy(R,C),
    S = #sum { V : output(R,C,V) }.
```

refinements.response:
**ASP solution**

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Domain
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R)      :- R = 0..15.
col(C)      :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Helper: a clue (non‑zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
clue(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Row extremes – safe aggregates (note the extra
%    column predicate in the head!)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(R, Cmin) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmin = #min { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmin).
row_max(R, Cmax) :- row(R), clue(R,C,V), Cmax = #max { C : clue(R,C,V) }, col(Cmax).
row_len(R, Len)  :- row(R), row_max(R, Cmax), row_min(R, Cmin),
                    Len = #int { Cmax - Cmin }, col(Cmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Column extremes
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_min(Rmin,C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmin = #min { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmin).
col_max(Rmax,C) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), Rmax = #max { R : clue(R,C,V) }, row(Rmax).
col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C) :- col(C), col_max(Rmax,C), col_min(Rmin,C),
                       Len = #int { Rmax - Rmin }, row(Rmin).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Central row / column (the “plus‑sign”)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_len(MaxLen,Rmax) :- row(Rmax), row_len(Rmax,Len), Len = #max { Len : row_len(R,Len) }.
central_row(R)      :- row(R), row_len(R,Len), Len = #max { Len : row_len(R,Len) }.

max_col_len(MaxLen,Cmax) :- col(Cmax), col_len(Rmin,Rmax,Cmax), Len = #max { Len : col_len(Rmin,Rmax,Cmax) }.
central_col(C)      :- col(C), col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C), Len = #max { Len : col_len(Rmin,Rmax,C) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior cells (inside the plus‑sign but not on the
%    central row or column)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    not central_row(R),
    not central_col(C),
    row_min(R,Cmin), row_max(R,Cmax),
    C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Colour of a column – the maximal colour that occurs
%    in that column (safe aggregate – column appears positively)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
col_max_colour(C,V) :- col(C), clue(R,C,V), V = #max { V2 : clue(R2,C,V2) }, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per cell – the allowed set decides
%    which colour is allowed.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% a) cells on the central row or column keep their clue
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    clue(R,C,V), V != 0,
    (central_row(R) ; central_col(C)),
    row(R), col(C).

% b) interior cells take the maximal colour of their column
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    interior(R,C),
    col_max_colour(C,V),
    row(R), col(C).

% c) all other cells stay empty
allowed(R,C,0) :-
    not clue(R,C,_),
    not interior(R,C),
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :-
    row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. No other statements – the program produces exactly one
%     answer set which matches the specification.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions
% ------------------------------------------------------------
row(R) :- R = 0..15.
col(C) :- C = 0..15.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Centre row and column (the “plus‑sign” cross)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
on_centre_row(R,C) :- R = 5, col(C).
on_centre_col(R,C) :- col(C) = 5, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Colour of the centre cell (the clue at (5,5))
% ------------------------------------------------------------
centre_colour(Cc) :- input(5,5,Cc), Cc != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Secondary colour – the smallest colour that differs from the centre colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
secondary_colour(Sc) :-
    centre_colour(Cc),
    colour(Sc),
    Sc != Cc,
    Sc = #min { X : colour(X), X != Cc, true }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Allowed colour for each cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    on_centre_row(R,C),
    centre_colour(Cc),
    V = Cc.

allowed(R,C,V) :-
    on_centre_col(R,C),
    centre_colour(Cc),
    V = Cc.

allowed(R,C,V) :-
    row(R),
    col(C),
    not on_centre_row(R,C),
    not on_centre_col(R,C),
    secondary_colour(Sc),
    not input(R,C,Sc),
    V = 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Exactly one colour per cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : colour(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% End of program
